text: An employee received an email with an attached invoice document that requested macros to be enabled before viewing.
A finance user opened a password-protected archive received by email containing a malicious spreadsheet
Multiple users reported a payroll-themed email carring a document that launched suspicious processes after opening.
An HR employee received a resume attachment that executed an unexpected command after being opend.
A compressed attachment deliverd through email extracted an executable that contacted an externa; server.
Security monitoring detected a malicious Office attachment attempting to launch a script interpreter.
An email attachment disguised as a shipping receipt initiated suspicious activity shortly after it was opened.
A target phishing email contained a document with embedded content that attempted to execute malware.
PowerShell was launched with an encoded command from a user workstation.
A shell script executed automatically after a suspicious archive was extracted.
An endpoint executed a command interpreter to download additinal payloads from a remote host.
The EDR platform detected a batch script spawning multiple child processes.
Python was used to executed a sequence of system commands outside normal administrative activity.
A script interpreter launched from Microsoft Office immediately after a document was opened.
Command execution activity originated from a temporary directory with obfuscated arguments.
Encoded PowerShell commands attempted to disable endpoint protection before continuing execution.
A dormant employee account successfully authenicated to a sensitive internal application after serveral onths of inactivity.
Administrative credentials were used from anew geographic location during non-business hours.
A privileged service account logged into systems it had never previously accessed.
Authentication logs showed repeated successful access using legitimate VPN credentials fro an unfamiliar device.
An account normally used only for backups authenticated interactively on a workstation.
Security monitoring identified successful access with a desabled employee's credentials before the account was revoked.
A shared administrator account acessed multiple production servers in rapid succession.
A workstation initiated Remote Desktop connections to several internal servers within minutes.
An administatior account established SMB sessions scross multiple hosts shortly after initial access.
SSH connections originated from an engineering workstation to systerm outside its normal management scope.
Remote management tools were used to execute commands on several endpoints from a single account.
Windows Remote Management traffic increased significantly between internal servers overnight.
Multiple hosts received remote administrative session shortly after suspicious autherntication events.
A compromised endpoint intitiated lateral movement using remote service protocols to additional systems.
Invoice attachment executed immediately after opening.
The email referenced an overdue payment and included a Word document that prompted the recipient to enable editing before embedded content attempted to run.
Outlook delivered a targeted message with a ZIP attachment containing a dishuised executable.
User reported a suspicious contract document receved from an external sender.
Security telemetry showed an Office attachment spawning a chlid process that connected to an external IP moments after the document was opened.
cmd.exe launched from an unusual directory.
PowerShell downloaded and executed a remote script using an encoded command line.
A Bash shell was invoked by a scheduled task shortly after an archive was extracted onto the server.
Script execution detected.
Python automation unexpectedly executed operating system commands that created new local users and modified scheduled tasks.
A valid VPN account logged in from a previously unseen device.
Okta logs showed successful authentication using legtimate credentials immediately after repeated failed login attempts.
Shared administrator account used overnight.
A service account authenticated to serveral production systems outside its established baseline without triggering MFA challenges.
Legitimate credentials were used to access cloud resources from a foreign location.
RDP session established to an internal server.
SSH was used to access multiple Linux hosts from a workstation that normally never performs administration.
Remote service enabled lateral movement between serveral Windows servers using SMB administrative shares.
Unexpected WinRM connections detected.
PcExec was observed executing commands remotely across multiple endpoints shortly after a privileged account authenticated successfully.
